tweets.text:
Fun new information discovery from Poet OSINT community:

Seems likely that $POET / $SIVE are going to power a Top-3 hyperscaler (either Amazon, Microsoft, Google). 

Given a Linkedin update from Ankur Singla (CEO of Lumilens). 

Who stated their customer is one of the top 3 hyperscalers with their post focusing on CPO/NPO. 

With that clue, seems more likely the Sivers CW DFB light source path over other EML suppliers given it's CPO Scale Out/NPO. 

If you don't remember, Sivers is the laser supplier to Poet. And Poet has purchase agreements with Lumilens.

Always fun to find major potential breadcrumbs in the wild before they're officially confirmed. (Disclosure, long Sive)

These rules

One of my core thesis this cycle is Ungated, Open and Decentralized AI

The thesis led me to Bittensor ecosystem early 2025

I've stuck around since then and curated a subnet portfolio out of genuine curiosity and belief that they're working on something very interesting

Recently, we just had one of the biggest updates to the Network/Tokenomics

And I've made a big change to my portfolio

Sharing the full thesis in The State of Bittensor Q2/2026, breaking down the changes, where we are, where do we go from here, and most importantly what I'm doing about it

Link below ↓

tweets.text:
A while back I made the mistake of trading something I’d never traded before.

So here’s an ignorant question… What’s actually stopping Bitcoin from going back to 35k?
tweets.full_text:
A while back I made the mistake of trading something I’d never traded before.

So here’s an ignorant question… What’s actually stopping Bitcoin from going back to 35k?

tweets.text: We have been fci loopin since the start of ‘22
tweets.quoted_tweet_text:
FCI Loop. 

Inflation up -> Fed hawkish -> rates up -> dollar stronger -> stocks correct. If allowed to play out for 3-6 months, inflation solved. 

But, stocks down -> bonds bid -> dollar reverses -> stocks moon -> story continues endlessly. 

We are about to repeat this loop.
tweets.full_text: We have been fci loopin since the start of ‘22

tweets.text: @fishingAssassin What’s the trade?
tweets.full_text: @fishingAssassin What’s the trade?

tweets.text:
Let me understand this right…

The current narrative is sell the AI capex payer because spend depresses FCF and buybacks. Buy the recipient because that same capex becomes revenue, margin, backlog, and scarcity pricing.

So if hyperscalers cut capex, they can rip and the bottlenecks can puke. I’ve honestly sat here for a while thinking about this… have we created a market where AI capex is heads I win, tails I win?

Capex up? Bottlenecks rally and drag the market higher.

Capex down? Hyperscalers get the FCF/buyback story back and drag the market higher.

thread_tweets.full_text:
Let me understand this right…

The current narrative is sell the AI capex payer because spend depresses FCF and buybacks. Buy the recipient because that same capex becomes revenue, margin, backlog, and scarcity pricing.

So if hyperscalers cut capex, they can rip and the bottlenecks can puke. I’ve honestly sat here for a while thinking about this… have we created a market where AI capex is heads I win, tails I win?

Capex up? Bottlenecks rally and drag the market higher.

Capex down? Hyperscalers get the FCF/buyback story back and drag the market higher.

thread_tweets.text:
Obviously the catch is that this only works if capex cuts are read as discipline, not AI demand related.

And don’t hit me with the ohhh fed you’re a perma bull. My best year was 2022 - please let that sink in. I am aware that at some point the tide will turn and this market will do what it did on the way up, but in reverse.
thread_tweets.full_text:
Obviously the catch is that this only works if capex cuts are read as discipline, not AI demand related.

And don’t hit me with the ohhh fed you’re a perma bull. My best year was 2022 - please let that sink in. I am aware that at some point the tide will turn and this market will do what it did on the way up, but in reverse.

thread_tweets.text:
So the bear case is if capex cuts (if/when they happen) are read as discipline and start being read as we overbuilt/ROI isn’t there/demand is weaker than expected…

But until that point, index literally has a built in AI hedge, capex up - bottlenecks lead, capex down - hyperscalers lead. And any pukes will be created by positioning/VaR shocks..

tweets.full_text:
Let me understand this right…

The current narrative is sell the AI capex payer because spend depresses FCF and buybacks. Buy the recipient because that same capex becomes revenue, margin, backlog, and scarcity pricing.

So if hyperscalers cut capex, they can rip and the bottlenecks can puke. I’ve honestly sat here for a while thinking about this… have we created a market where AI capex is heads I win, tails I win?

Capex up? Bottlenecks rally and drag the market higher.

Capex down? Hyperscalers get the FCF/buyback story back and drag the market higher.

Obviously the catch is that this only works if capex cuts are read as discipline, not AI demand related.

And don’t hit me with the ohhh fed you’re a perma bull. My best year was 2022 - please let that sink in. I am aware that at some point the tide will turn and this market will do what it did on the way up, but in reverse.

So the bear case is if capex cuts (if/when they happen) are read as discipline and start being read as we overbuilt/ROI isn’t there/demand is weaker than expected…

But until that point, index literally has a built in AI hedge, capex up - bottlenecks lead, capex down - hyperscalers lead. And any pukes will be created by positioning/VaR shocks..

*until that point*

the pain trade remains https://t.co/8MriiwrvMi
